content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN toys and each of his toys has its beauty - with the iii-th toy having aia_iai​ units of beauty. Chef is also obsessed with number XXX, so he wants to combine the beauty of 4 different of his toys to be exactly XXX. The beauty of combined toys is their product. Chef is doing his homework and doesn't have time to play with his toys, so he asks you to help him find 4 different toys whose combined beauty is exactly XXX. If there are multiple answers print the lexicographically minimal solution.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains two integers NNN and XXX.
Second line of each testcase contains NNN integers representing the beauty of each toys: aia_iai​.


Output Format
For each testcase, if the solution exists - output four pair-wise different space-separated integers i,j,k,li,j,k,li,j,k,l such that combined beauty of ai,aj,ak,ala_i, a_j, a_k, a_lai​,aj​,ak​,al​ is exactly XXX. If there are multiple answers output the lexicographically smallest solution.


If the solution doesn't exist - output −1-1−1.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200020002000
30 points : The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100100100.
20 points : XXX is a prime number

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case we can pick numbers: 3,2,2,23, 2, 2, 23,2,2,2 and their product is 242424 as requested. Their indices are obviously 1,2,3,61, 2, 3, 61,2,3,6.
In the second test case we cannot pick four different indices so the product of the elements with those indices is 242424.
